description_long: This structure is a traditional trullo, characterized by its thick, rough stone walls and a large, prominent conical dome made of reddish-brown tiles. The front facade features a small, arched doorway flanked by two columns and topped with a simple arch, as well as two small arched windows and a circular window above the entrance. The stonework is irregular and rustic, giving the building a historic and hand-crafted appearance. The roof is constructed from overlapping stone tiles, forming a smooth, rounded dome without a decorative pinnacle. The rear of the building is more austere, with the stonework continuing uninterrupted and no visible windows or doors. The overall form is compact and solid, with a rectangular base supporting the distinctive domed roof.
description: A rustic stone trullo with a large conical dome and a small arched entrance, featuring rough stone walls and a reddish-brown tiled roof.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the thick stone walls and the full curvature of the conical dome, with the roof tiles clearly visible.
description_view_1: The front view displays the small arched doorway, two flanking columns, and three arched windows, emphasizing the building's entrance and facade details.
description_view_2: This rear-side view shows the uninterrupted stone wall and the dome's curvature, with no visible openings or decorative elements.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a plain stone wall and the rear curvature of the dome, with the entrance arch partially visible on the side.
